summary: Tested crowd-sourced parking status feature: backend APIs (GET/POST /api/poi/parking-status) and frontend code in NavigationView.tsx. Found and fixed a CRITICAL syntax error in NavigationView.tsx introduced when inserting the new parking status useEffect. All 15 backend tests pass. App loads without errors after fix. Frontend parking status UI code is correctly implemented (states, useEffect, submitParkingStatus, 4 colored buttons with data-testid attributes). POI popup UI testing limited to code review since the popup requires actual map POI marker click (needs HERE API geolocation).
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL (FIXED): NavigationView.tsx lines 665-668 had orphaned code from the HOS violation useEffect. When the parking status useEffect was inserted, the main agent accidentally replaced the beginning of the existing `useEffect(() => { if (hasViolation && !lastViolationRef.current) {` — leaving only the tail `speak(...)`, `}`, `lastViolationRef.current = hasViolation;`, `}, [hasViolation]);` dangling outside any function context. This caused a Babel parse error (`Unexpected token (668:2)`) that broke the entire NavigationView component. FIX: Restored the complete violation useEffect with proper `useEffect(() => { if (hasViolation && !lastViolationRef.current) { ... }` opening.
action_items: The fix for the HOS violation useEffect has already been applied. Main agent should verify the HOS violation alert logic is complete (the restored useEffect matches original intent: 'if (hasViolation && !lastViolationRef.current)' check before speaking).

Frontend POI popup testing was code-review only (not Playwright-interactive) since the popup requires clicking a POI marker on the Leaflet map which needs HERE Maps geolocation. Manual test recommended: click any POI marker in Navigation view to verify the Parking Status section appears with 4 buttons.

success_rate.frontend: 85% (app loads, code verified, interactive popup not tested due to map interaction requirement)

context_for_next_testing_agent: App at http://localhost:8001. Single Express+Vite server (no separate backend/frontend). Parking status backend APIs fully tested. The POI popup (which shows the parking status section) only appears when a POI marker is clicked on the Leaflet map in Navigation view — requires geolocation or map pan to load POI markers from HERE Maps API. Backend test file: /app/tests/test_parking_status.py. No auth required.

test_results.syntax_error_fixed: PASS - HOS violation useEffect restored at lines 666-671
test_results.code_review_data_testid_parking_buttons: PASS - data-testid={`parking-status-${opt.key}`} at line 3884 generates all 4 IDs

rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE: The main agent used search-and-replace to insert the parking status useEffect. The search string used to find the insertion point was the beginning of the HOS violation useEffect (`useEffect(() => {\n    if (hasViolation && !lastViolationRef.current) {`). The replacement only added the new parking status code but did NOT include the original violation useEffect opening lines, removing them from the file. This left lines 665-668 (the tail of the violation useEffect) as orphaned/unreachable code after the new useEffect's closing `}`, causing a Babel parse error. MITIGATION: Before inserting code with search-replace around useEffect boundaries, always ensure the full original block is preserved in the replacement.
